name: Schneider
tagline: Purpose-driven family foundation for chronic-illness research

scenario.event: The family's core European pharma large-cap announces it is shutting down its neurodegenerative-disease research division to refocus on higher-margin lifestyle drugs.
scenario.dnaHook: Hubertus & Carmen hold large-cap pharma explicitly to fund the fight against their daughter Chloe's neurodegenerative condition; a holding that defunds that research is, in his words, a 'personal betrayal' and must be flagged for divestment.

name: Huber
tagline: Environmentalist focused on South American reforestation

scenario.event: A major global consumer-goods conglomerate announces it is completely cutting off suppliers tied to palm-oil deforestation and launching a multi-million-dollar foundation to reforest the Brazilian rainforest.
scenario.dnaHook: Huber's portfolio is built around environmental impact and South American reforestation; this corporate shift is a values-aligned opportunity to tilt toward the issuer.

name: Räber
tagline: Conservative Swiss couple, averse to US technology exposure

scenario.event: The CIO issues a global Tactical Asset Allocation update: reduce defensive value (Consumer Staples & Health Care) to fund a new position in mega-cap US technology.
scenario.dnaHook: The Räbers are conservative and explicitly averse to US tech; a TAA-driven rotation into mega-cap US technology clashes with their stated comfort and must be reconciled with the mandate.

name: Ammann
tagline: Swiss entrepreneur — reputational risk is financial risk

scenario.event: A labour-exploitation scandal breaks at a consumer brand held in the portfolio, drawing intense media coverage.
scenario.dnaHook: Julian is a public advocate for ethical corporate leadership; he treats reputational risk as financial risk and cannot be associated with a labour-exploitation scandal.
